item.title: For Once, We Fight With an Equal Ally
item.description: Israel is the rare U.S. ally that pulls its weight, shares the risk and contributes to victory.

item.title: The Unlikely Alliance to Save East Palestine, Ohio
item.description: In Ohio after a toxic chemical accident, finding common ground in the country’s health wars.

item.title: A.I. Is Coming for Politics
item.description: How ready are we?

item.title: It’s Cruel to Take Away People’s H.I.V. Medication
item.description: States are rolling back coverage for lifesaving medications.

item.title: Trump Bullied Allies. Now They’re Hard to Find.
item.description: Readers discuss developments in the Iran war, including strains with allies. Also: The high cost of opera, not just at the Met.

item.title: What Democrats Should Relearn From Obama
item.description: How to fix the Democratic Party’s toxic brand.

item.title: Trump’s Dangerous Lack of a Strategy in Iran
item.description: The early reality of the Iran war is not cooperating with the president’s bluster.

item.title: The Scrappy Mayor Showing Democrats How It’s Done
item.description: Democrats can’t just sit back and expect the prevailing political winds to produce a blue wave.

item.title: How Trump Destroyed the Aura of the Wartime President
item.description: Even as he’s wrecking American institutions, he is revealing the limits of his cultural influence.

item.title: The Trump Administration Just Gave Live Nation the Gift of a Lifetime
item.description: The Justice Department’s Live Nation settlement took everyone by surprise, even its lead counsel.

item.title: Mass Shooters Are Getting Younger and More Online
item.description: The violence is not a means to an end. It is the end.

item.title: Why Affordability Still Hurts Democrats
item.description: Affordability is Democrats’ new buzzword, the Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson says. But what’s their plan to actually lower prices? She explains on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Trump Is Trying to Sell War Like Steaks
item.description: The president is approaching the war in Iran as if he’s selling another Trump product, the writer E.J. Dionne Jr. says on “The Opinions.” But “selling war is a very serious business,” he adds, unlike selling Trump Steaks or Trump University.

item.title: Dubai Was Not Built for War
item.description: The new global city under fire.

item.title: I Was an F.B.I. Agent for 25 Years. Kash Patel Is Playing a Dangerous Game.
item.description: Let’s hope it doesn’t take another Sept. 11 to snap the F.B.I.’s leadership into action.

item.title: Modern Language and the ‘Cult of the Casual’
item.description: Is saying “like” and “sort of” the equivalent of verbal bubble wrap? John McWhorter, an Opinion writer and a linguistics professor at Columbia, argues yes, because they soften the sharp edges of what we really mean. He explains the larger implications of the language we might not even realize we’re using.

item.title: I Predicted the 2008 Financial Crisis. What Is Coming May Be Worse.
item.description: Richard Bookstaber warns that what appears to be disparate forces adding risk to our economy are all part of one deeply connected system.

item.title: The Choice Bisexual Men Are Asked to Make
item.description: As my mother saw it, bisexuality meant I had a choice. She thought I simply kept making the wrong one.

item.title: Weighing Risks vs. Gains in Iran
item.description: Readers respond to a column by Nicholas Kristof. Also: Artificial intelligence pitfalls in school.

item.title: ‘One Battle After Another’ Was ‘Totally Meh’: 3 Opinion Writers Size Up the Oscars
item.description: What did the academy get right? Wrong? What was just weird? Three culture fans discuss Hollywood’s biggest night.

item.title: ‘This Spells Real Trouble for Republicans’
item.description: The Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson joins E.J. Dionne Jr. and Robert Siegel to discuss Trump’s falling approval rating and what it portends for November.

item.title: Why I’m Suing Grammarly
item.description: A tech company made a deepfake of my mind. I’m fighting back.

item.title: We Have Reached End-Stage Polarization
item.description: The omnipresence of Trump should open our eyes.

item.title: A Weakened Iran Won’t Transform the Middle East
item.description: A defeated Iran will not produce the transformation of the Middle East sought by many.

item.title: China’s Plan to Spur Consumer Spending Is a Mirage
item.description: China may never be able to realize its longtime promise to shift away from an overreliance on exports.

item.title: The Rise of Anxiety Cinema
item.description: Some movies make us laugh or cry or feel scared. A new wave of jittery cinema seems designed to make us feel incredibly stressed out.

item.title: Timothée Chalamet Was Supposed to Be a New Kind of Leading Man
item.description: He’s no longer that heart-on-his-sleeve sensitive soft boy many people fell for.

item.title: The Paradox of Lebanon
item.description: Lebanon needs to be rid of Hezbollah, but not at the cost of Israeli occupation.

item.title: ‘One Battle After Another’ Is Just a Movie. Resistance Is Real.
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay about the Paul Thomas Anderson movie “One Battle After Another” by the daughter of two Weatherman. Also: Will we be around to unearth a time capsule?

item.title: Why Minnesota Matters More Than Iran for America’s Future
item.description: Moms donating their breast milk to strangers, dads taking someone else’s kids to school: Minnesotans showed a basic human impulse to look out for their neighbors.

item.title: Small Talk Isn’t Small
item.description: People with whom you make small talk are made aware that for at least one moment in their lives, they have a safe home with you, a place where they are welcome just as they are.

item.title: The Harm of Banning Care for Trans Youth
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay that argued that medical associations have lost credibility over their approach to care for transgender youth.

item.title: Trump Wins When Oscar Winners Stay Silent
item.description: Right now it does matter a lot whether actors can find the right words.

item.title: Any Way You Look at It, Netanyahu Wins
item.description: The war in Iran is a victory for the Israeli prime minister.

item.title: Is There Method to Trump’s Madness?
item.description: Don’t hold your breath.

item.title: The Lost Horizon of John and Carolyn
item.description: “Love Story” is restoring Camelot’s sheen, tarnished by R.F.K. Jr.

item.title: I Love the Movies. Here’s How to Save Them.
item.description: The industry is still recovering from the upheavals of the pandemic era. But there’s one simple step to take right now that can ensure a bright future ahead.

item.title: Does Trump Risk Turning America Into a Rogue State?
item.description: The United States helped create safeguards to limit the brutality of war, and now I fear President Trump is dismantling them.

item.title: What Trump Didn’t Know About Iran
item.description: Ali Vaez, the Iran project director at the International Crisis Group, explains the history of miscalculations that led to the war in Iran.

item.title: Social Media Isn’t Just Speech. It’s Also a Defective, Hazardous Product.
item.description: Don’t let the First Amendment arguments fool you.

item.title: The Political Cost of Trump’s War
item.description: The Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson joins E.J. Dionne Jr. and Robert Siegel to discuss Trump’s falling approval rating and what it portends for November.

item.title: Europe Is in Great Danger. It Has Itself to Blame.
item.description: European politicians have colluded in their dependency on a rogue America.

item.title: What Are We Living Through in Trump 2.0? Here Are 3 Possibilities.
item.description: On the modes of authoritarian crisis, more of the same and constitutional regime change.

item.title: Mussolini Would Have Loved Trump’s Ballroom
item.description: Trump’s plans for Washington bring to mind what Mussolini did — and tried to do — to Rome.

item.title: The Death Penalty Is Indefensible. It’s Also on the Rise.
item.description: The surge in capital punishment is a cruel and unjust development.

item.title: The Trump Administration Floats a New Way to Humiliate the Legal Profession
item.description: The consequences would be grave.
